briefdescription.para: Enable verbose EDID parsing debug output (0=disabled, 1=enabled)
This function reads the full EDID (base block + all extensions) from the connected monitor, validates header and checksum, and stores it in the AVCaps structure's embedded buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is a pointer to XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure where EDID will be stored in AVCaps->EdidBuffer and extension count in AVCaps->extensions_count.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if all EDID blocks read and Base block validated successfully. XST_FAILURE if instance/structure pointer invalid, monitor not connected, EDID read fails, or EDID header/checksum invalid.
This function validates base EDID header and checksum. Extension count is limited to MAX_EXTENSIONS_SUPPORTED. Each EDID block is 128 bytes.

briefdescription.para: This function parses complete Audio/Video capabilities and timings from embedded EDID buffer by aggregating data from base EDID, CTA-861, and DisplayID extension blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure with EDID already loaded in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. On return, AVCaps->checksum_failures will contain the count of extension blocks with checksum errors (0 if all blocks valid).

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if all blocks are parsed successfully XST_FAILURE if any error is encountered

EDID must be loaded via XDpTxSs_get_full_edid() before calling. Maximum SAD_count is limited to MAX_SAD_COUNT. Maximum timing count is limited to EDID_MAX_TIMINGS. CTA-861 blocks are optional (success returned if none found). DisplayID blocks are optional (success returned if none found). Speaker allocation is OR'd across all CTA-861 blocks. If arrays fill up, parsing continues but additional entries ignored. Video timings are parsed by calling XDpTxSs_GetVideoTimingsFromEdid() internally and stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] in priority order. Parse statistics from both CTA-861 and DisplayID blocks are accumulated and reported in the summary.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam *Timing, u16 HActive, u16 VActive, u8 RefreshRate)
memberdef.name: XDpTxSs_FillEstablishedTiming

briefdescription.para: Helper function to fill established timing with known values and zero-fill unavailable parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure to populate.
is the horizontal active pixels.
is the vertical active lines.
is the refresh rate in Hz.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam *Timing, u16 HActive, u16 VActive, u8 AspectRatio, u8 RefreshRate)
memberdef.name: XDpTxSs_FillStandardTiming

briefdescription.para: Helper function to fill standard timing with known values and zero-fill unavailable parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure to populate.
is the horizontal active pixels.
is the vertical active lines.
is the aspect ratio code (0-3).
is the refresh rate in Hz.

briefdescription.para: This function converts XV_VidC_TimingParam to XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam format.

parameterdescription.para: is the source timing parameter structure.
is the destination XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure.

briefdescription.para: This function validates all DisplayID 2.0 extension blocks by checking checksums.

detaileddescription.para: Called once to count total and invalid DisplayID blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the AV capabilities structure with EDID in AVCaps->EdidBuffer.

briefdescription.para: This function parses DisplayID 2.0 extension blocks and extracts Type VII detailed timings based on the specified category filter (Preferred/Native/Remaining).

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes that blocks have been pre-validated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the AV capabilities structure with EDID in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. Timings will be stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] and AVCaps->TimingCount is updated with the number of timings added.

specifies which timing category to extract:
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_PREFERRED: Timings with Preferred flag (Options bit 7)
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_NATIVE: Timings with Native flag (Options bit 6, Preferred clear)
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_REMAINING: All other timings (both flags clear)

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if parsing completed. XST_FAILURE if AVCaps pointer is invalid.
This function does NOT validate checksums - caller must validate first. DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Timing Descriptor Structure (20 bytes)

briefdescription.para: This function extracts video timings from an EDID structure and populates the timing list within an AV capabilities structure in priority order.

detaileddescription.para: Index 0 contains the highest priority timing (preferred timing) from the sink.

Priority order (EdidTimingPriority enum):
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_PREFERRED - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Preferred
PRIORITY_BASE_DTD1_PREFERRED - Base EDID DTD #1 (EDID spec mandated preferred)
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_NATIVE - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Native
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_DTD_OTHER - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII (remaining DTDs)
PRIORITY_BASE_DTD_OTHER - Base EDID DTD #2-4
PRIORITY_STANDARD_TIMINGS - Base EDID Standard Timings
PRIORITY_ESTABLISHED_TIMINGS - Base EDID Established Timings

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure with EDID loaded in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. Timings are stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] (max EDID_MAX_TIMINGS) and count in AVCaps->TimingCount.

simplesect.para: This function is typically called internally by XDpTxSs_get_sink_AV_Capabilities(). The caller must ensure the base EDID header and checksum have been validated before calling. The AVCaps structure must be allocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: This function prints debug information about a video timing.

parameterdescription.para: is the video timing structure to print.
is the index number to display.
